user.title: Closest Intent
user.description: Fuzzy intent matcher. Garbled STT output in, actual intent out. Defaults are sensible -- start with them and tune later via the options.

data_description.threshold: Minimum fuzzy-match score (0-100). Higher = stricter. Below this, the original text is forwarded unchanged to the fallback agent.
data_description.slot_threshold: Minimum score for resolving a captured slot value against its known list. Lower if entity names are frequently misunderstood.
data_description.expansion_cap: Maximum number of surface forms generated per pattern. Bounds the alternation/optional explosion. 0 disables expansion entirely.
data_description.denylist: Intent names to exclude from matching. Useful when a built-in or imported intent collides with your own.
data_description.slot_extraction: Substitute extracted slot values into the canonical sentence forwarded to Hassil. Turn off to only correct slot-less phrases.
data_description.include_builtins: Also fuzzy-match against Home Assistant's built-in intents (HassTurnOn etc.). Off by default; prefer the allowlist below.
data_description.builtin_allowlist: Specific built-in intent names to always consider, even when the include-builtins toggle above is off.
data_description.fallback_agent: Agent consulted only if no intent matches and Hassil also fails. Default is Hassil itself (i.e. no fallback). Set to an LLM agent if you want one.
data_description.startup_self_check: On startup, verify that each custom intent's perfect-input still routes to itself. Raises a repair issue if clashes are found. Recommended on.
data_description.suggestions: When nothing clears the match threshold, respond with 'did you mean ...?' naming the closest near-miss(es) instead of forwarding to the fallback agent.

init.title: Closest Intent options
init.description: Changes apply immediately -- no restart needed.

data_description.threshold: Minimum fuzzy-match score (0-100). Higher = stricter. Below this, the original text is forwarded unchanged to the fallback agent.

data_description.fallback_agent: Agent consulted only if no intent matches and Hassil also fails. Default is Hassil itself (i.e. no fallback). Set to an LLM agent if you want one.
data_description.startup_self_check: On startup, verify that each custom intent's perfect-input still routes to itself. Raises a repair issue if clashes are found. Recommended on.

self_check_clashes.title: Closest Intent: {count} clashing custom intent(s)
self_check_clashes.description:
Closest Intent's startup self-check feeds each of your custom intents' canonical sentence through the fuzzy matcher. For **{count}** intent(s) in language `{language}`, the matcher routed these perfect inputs to a *different* intent rather than the one it came from.

This usually means two custom intents have fuzzy-overlapping patterns and one is consistently shadowing the other on perfect input.

**Clashes, grouped by the intent that should have been matched:**

{details}

**Before opening an issue about these**, please try to determine if these matches are legitimate fuzzy-matching behavior errors, or configuration errors. Use the `parse_sentence` and `dump_candidates` debug tools.

If you believe the errors are genuinely bugs in Closest Intent, please open an issue with as much detail as you can provide! In the meantime, here's some stop-gap measure you can take:

- Narrow the source pattern: add more anchoring words so it stops matching the shadowing input.
- Add the shadowing intent's name to `denylist` if you do not actually use it.
- Set `startup_self_check: false` if the clashes are acceptable for your usage.
